generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance ActuatorControlSystem : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Cyber-physical control system for autonomous decision-making in digital twin-optimized cyber-physical processes (e.g., ISO 23247-1:2021 manufacturing frameworks)"
]

instance EdgeProcessingUnit : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Low-power edge device for real-time processing (NVIDIA Jetson) handling low-latency data streams in edge-based digital twins (ISO 23247 adaptations)"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance LiDARPointCloudSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-resolution LiDAR sensor for 3D environmental mapping in automotive applications (real-time point cloud clustering/tracking)"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance MotorActuationSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Motor position/velocity sensor for vehicle control systems (real-time actuation feedback)"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorActuationData
]

instance SteeringServoSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Steering angle sensor for autonomous vehicle control (real-time steering position tracking)"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringPositionData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Comprehensive vehicle state sensor (speed, position, acceleration) for real-time monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARPointCloudSensor
]

instance MotorActuationData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorActuationSensor
]

instance SteeringPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringServoSensor
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance ProcessedClusteringData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed point cloud clustering/tracking results for object detection in digital twin visualization"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance FeatureExtractionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Extracted feature vectors (e.g., driving patterns, control trajectory) for predictive analytics"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorActuationData, SteeringPositionData, VehicleStateData
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance DigitalTwinGeometryModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "3D geometric representation of physical system components (automotive chassis, manufacturing equipment) for visualization (NVIDIA Omniverse)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo NVIDIAOmniversePlatform
]

instance PhysicsSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "High-fidelity physics-based simulation (ANSYS TwinWorks/COMSOL) for cyber-physical interaction modeling"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ANSYSTwinWorks, COMSOLMultiphysics
]

instance ProcessSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Process simulation model for manufacturing operations (ISO 23247-1 framework) with parametric tuning capabilities"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SiemensMindSphere
]

instance FailurePredictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Machine learning model (TensorFlow/PyTorch) for failure prediction (battery degradation, component wear)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TensorFlowFramework, ScikitLearn
]

instance AnomalyDetectionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Detected anomalies in cyber-physical processes (unsupervised learning outputs from Softw. Syst. Model 2025)"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ProcessedClusteringData, FeatureExtractionData
]

instance RealTimeStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Synchronized real-time state representation between physical and digital twins (ISO 23247-based synchronization)"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorActuationData, SteeringPositionData, VehicleStateData
]

instance HistoricalDataArchive : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Archived historical data (AWS S3/Azure Data Lake) for long-term analysis and trend forecasting"
    DTDFVocab:fromData FeatureExtractionData
]

// C11: Enablers
instance NVIDIAOmniversePlatform : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time 3D simulation and visualization platform for physical-digital convergence (automotive/aerospace applications)"
    DTDFVocab:enables DigitalTwinGeometryModel, InteractiveVisualizationService
]

instance SiemensMindSphere : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Cloud-based IoT platform with AI/ML integration for digital twin analytics (predictive maintenance)"
    DTDFVocab:enables ProcessSimulationModel, AnomalyDetectionService
]

instance ANSYSTwinWorks : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "High-fidelity physics-based simulation engine for cyber-physical interaction modeling"
    DTDFVocab:enables PhysicsSimulationModel, ControlFeedbackService
]

instance COMSOLMultiphysics : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Multiphysical coupling simulation platform for complex system interactions (e.g., battery thermal management)"
    DTDFVocab:enables PhysicsSimulationModel
]

instance TensorFlowFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Deep learning framework for predictive analytics (failure forecasting, scenario simulation)"
    DTDFVocab:enables FailurePredictionModel, ScenarioSimulationService
]

instance ScikitLearn : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Scikit-learn for classification/regression tasks in digital twin analytics"
    DTDFVocab:enables AnomalyDetectionService
]

instance ApacheCassandra : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "High-write, low-latency distributed database for edge digital twins (ISO 23247 edge computing adaptations)"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeStateData
]

instance Neo4jGraphDatabase : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Graph database for relationship-centric digital twin models (component dependencies in manufacturing)"
    DTDFVocab:enables ComponentDependencyService
]

instance OWLStandard : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Semantic web standard (ISO 30173:2023) for ontology-based interoperability"
    DTDFVocab:enables SemanticConsistencyService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous bidirectional data synchronization between physical and digital twins (ISO 23247-based frameworks)"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeStateData, AnomalyDetectionData
]

instance AnomalyDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "AI-driven anomaly detection using unsupervised learning (Softw. Syst. Model 2025) for cyber-physical process monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyDetectionInsight
]

    base:desc "Dynamic parameter tuning for physical system control (genetic algorithms, reinforcement learning) in edge digital twins"
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimizedParameterAction
]

instance FailurePredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Machine learning-based failure prediction (battery degradation, component wear) for predictive maintenance"
    DTDFVocab:provides FailurePredictionInsight
]

instance InteractiveVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "3D/4D visualization dashboards (Tableau/Power BI/Unity/Unreal Engine) for engineers and operators"
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationInsight
]

instance ARMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Augmented reality-guided maintenance (Microsoft HoloLens) for remote diagnostics and repairs"
    DTDFVocab:provides ARMaintenanceAction
]

instance ControlFeedbackService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Closed-loop control adaptation using PID/MPC controllers for autonomous decision-making"

]

instance ComponentDependencyService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Graph-based relationship analysis for component dependencies in manufacturing systems"
    DTDFVocab:provides DependencyInsight
]

instance ScenarioSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Virtual testing of cyber-physical interactions (collision avoidance, system stress testing)"
    DTDFVocab:provides ScenarioSimulationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions

    base:desc "Detected deviations in cyber-physical processes (e.g., battery degradation, vibration anomalies)"
]

    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of physical system parameters (e.g., actuator response times) for energy-efficient control"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

    base:desc "Predicted failure probabilities for components (e.g., automotive parts, manufacturing equipment)"
]

instance VisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [

]

    base:desc "Autonomous control commands (PID/MPC) for real-time system stabilization"

]

// C17: Environment
instance AutomotiveEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains LiDARPointCloudSensor, MotorActuationSensor, SteeringServoSensor, VehicleStateSensor,
                  DigitalTwinGeometryModel, PhysicsSimulationModel, InteractiveVisualizationService,
                  ARMaintenanceService, ControlFeedbackService
    base:desc "Automotive cyber-physical system environment with real-time digital twin integration"
]

instance IndustrialManufacturingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains ProcessSimulationModel, ParametricOptimizationService, FailurePredictionService,
                  ComponentDependencyService, SiemensMindSphere, ANSYSTwinWorks
    base:desc "Industrial IoT manufacturing environment with edge-based digital twin capabilities"
]

instance EdgeProcessingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains EdgeProcessingUnit, ApacheCassandra, Neo4jGraphDatabase,
                  OWLStandard
    base:desc "Edge computing infrastructure for low-latency digital twin operations"
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
  **Control Flows from Digital to Physical:**
  - **Command Types:**
    - **Control Signals:** Digital twin sends actuation commands (e.g., motor/steering servo adjustments) via the **Actuation FE** module to the physical vehicle.
    - **Reporting/Feedback:** Data Collecting FE and Reporting FE generate structured reports (e.g., ACC-related controls) but no explicit control commands are described for feedback loops.

  - **Control Message Formats:**
    - **Actuation FE:** Serialized commands (aligned with ROS2 message types) sent to physical actuators (e.g., motor/steering servos).
    - **Data Translation FE:** Ensures compatibility across message types/units (e.g., converting digital signals to physical actuator protocols).

  - **Validation and Authentication Mechanisms:**
    - **Not in Document:** No mention of authentication (e.g., TLS, digital signatures) or validation (e.g., checksums, rate limiting) for control messages.

  - **Feedback Systems:**
    - **Not Explicitly Described:** The architecture shows data flows but lacks feedback loops (e.g., how physical twin confirms command execution or reports errors).

  - **Conflict Resolution:**
    - **Not in Document:** No mechanisms for handling conflicts between digital commands and physical constraints (e.g., sensor limits, safety protocols).

  - **Command Execution Confirmation:**
    - **Not in Document:** No acknowledgments or status updates from the physical twin (e.g., ROS2 acknowledgments, success/failure flags).
  "
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces **strict time synchronization** between physical and digital components, mandating **sub-100 ms clock accuracy** for computation offloading and safety-critical vehicular applications. This requirement stems from hardware limitations and ensures precise temporal alignment for real-time data processing, actuation, and coordination across distributed entities (e.g., sensors, actuators, and ROS2 nodes). The system does not specify granularity for individual services, but the **100 ms latency threshold** is critical for maintaining deterministic behavior in the Physical Twin Entity, particularly for functions like Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking, where timing-sensitive operations are essential."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system’s architecture follows **ISO 23247 Reference Architecture (RA)**, with a **component-centric orchestration** pattern. Key integration details include:
  - **Entity-Based Deployment**: Software components are mapped to ISO 23247 entities (User, Digital Twin, Physical Twin) and implemented as **ROS2 packages**, each hosting one or more ROS2 nodes. This modularity enables **hardware-aware deployment** (e.g., FE-specific hardware allocation).
  - **Resource Management**: The Physical Twin Entity hosts **general automotive functionality** (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking) alongside device-specific resources (e.g., sensors, actuators), deviating from ISO 23247’s OME (Operations Management Entity). This deviation impacts **ownership and engineering processes** by distributing functionality across hardware components.
  - **Coordination Mechanisms**: ROS2’s **publish-subscribe model** underpins inter-FE communication, ensuring real-time data exchange. The absence of explicit orchestration tools (e.g., Kubernetes, Zephyr) implies **low-level coordination** via ROS2’s middleware."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The ISO 23247 framework does not explicitly detail **fidelity requirements**, **validation/verification methods**, or **uncertainty quantification** for the Digital Twin Entity. However, the **Functional Entities (FEs)** within the entity-based architecture implicitly imply a need for model accuracy, as they interface with the Observable Manufacturing Element (OME) and User Entity. While no specific error detection/correction mechanisms or quality assurance processes are mentioned, the standard’s emphasis on **real-time data integration** suggests reliance on automated validation pipelines (e.g., cross-checking sensor data with digital models). The absence of explicit documentation means no concrete methods (e.g., Monte Carlo simulations, statistical tests) or thresholds (e.g., error tolerance limits) are defined for ensuring model validity.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
  **Technical Network Infrastructure:**
  - **Communication Protocols:**
    - **Intra-SW:** ROS2 (ROS2-based middleware for inter-node communication).
    - **Inter-Entity:** Wi-Fi (UDP) for cross-entity (physical/digital) data transfer.

  - **Network Topology and Architecture:**
    - **On-vehicle Edge Deployment:** Sensors (LiDAR, motor/steering) and FE modules are co-located on the vehicle (physical twin).
    - **Cloud/Edge Hybrid:** Digital twin components (e.g., Visualization FE, Reporting FE) likely reside on a cloud/edge server for processing/visualization (not explicitly stated).
    - **ROS2 Network:** Localized for intra-vehicle SW components; Wi-Fi bridges to external entities (e.g., cloud services).

  - **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements:**
    - **High Latency Sensitivity:** Real-time visualization (e.g., 3D point cloud rendering) requires low-latency data streams (e.g., <100ms for LiDAR processing).
    - **Bandwidth:** LiDAR point clouds (high-resolution) and frequent updates (e.g., motor/steering data) demand significant bandwidth (e.g., 10–100 Mbps).

  - **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms:**
    - **Redundancy:** Not explicitly mentioned, but implied by FE modules (e.g., Actuation FE, Data Pre-Processing FE) for fault tolerance.
    - **Reliability:** ROS2’s built-in mechanisms (e.g., message retries, deadlines) ensure data integrity for critical paths.

  - **Security Protocols:**
    - **Data Translation FE:** Serializes/aligns data but no explicit encryption (e.g., TLS) or access controls for Wi-Fi/UDP traffic.
    - **No Formal Security:** No mention of authentication (e.g., IPsec), encryption, or intrusion detection for network communication.
  "
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
  **Deployment Infrastructure:**
  - **Hosting Platforms:**
    - **Edge Computing:** Physical twin sensors and FE modules deployed on-vehicle (edge) for low-latency processing.
    - **Cloud/Edge Hybrid:** Digital twin services (e.g., Visualization FE, Reporting FE) likely hosted on cloud/edge servers for scalability (not explicitly stated).

  - **Computational and Storage Requirements:**
    - **Edge:**
      - **Computational:** Real-time processing of LiDAR point clouds, clustering/tracking (e.g., GPU-accelerated FE modules).
      - **Storage:** Temporary storage for raw sensor data (e.g., LiDAR point clouds) and processed features (e.g., vehicle state trajectories).
    - **Cloud:**
      - **Computational:** Scalable processing for aggregated data (e.g., ACC reports, 3D visualizations).
      - **Storage:** Long-term storage for historical data (e.g., reporting FE archives).

  - **Scalability Mechanisms:**
    - **ROS2 Network:** Supports horizontal scaling for SW components (e.g., adding more LiDAR sensors).
    - **Cloud Services:** Auto-scaling for digital twin services (e.g., Reporting FE, Data Collecting FE) based on demand.

  - **Deployment Models Supported:**
    - **On-Premise/Edge:** Physical twin deployed on-vehicle (edge) for real-time control.
    - **Cloud-Based:** Digital twin services (e.g., visualization, reporting) deployed in the cloud for accessibility.

  - **Availability/Reliability Assurance:**
    - **Edge:** High availability via redundant FE modules (e.g., Actuation FE, Data Pre-Processing FE).
    - **Cloud:** Not explicitly detailed, but implied to use cloud SLAs (e.g., 99.9% uptime) for digital twin services.
    - **No Formal Redundancy:** No mention of failover mechanisms (e.g., backup servers) for cloud components.
  "
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "
The **data ownership and privacy** characteristic for a Digital Twin system in the context of **v2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) services** (as per **3GPP TS 22.185: Release 15, 2018**) and **functional safety applications** (ISO 26262-1:2018) must adhere to strict governance frameworks to ensure compliance with data protection regulations. While the documents do not explicitly detail explicit ownership policies, the **3GPP TS 22.185** specifies **service requirements for v2X communications**, which inherently involve **data sharing agreements** between vehicles, infrastructure, and service providers. These agreements must enforce **consent management** (e.g., explicit user authorization for data collection and processing) to comply with privacy laws like GDPR, ensuring transparency in data usage.

For **data anonymization**, the absence of explicit standards in the provided documents implies reliance on **ISO 26262-1:2018’s functional safety requirements**, which mandate **risk-based anonymization techniques** to prevent unauthorized access to sensitive vehicle or user data. The **ISO 15622 (Adaptive Cruise Control, ACC)** standard does not address privacy, but its emphasis on **real-time system reliability** suggests that any data shared must be processed under **strict access control mechanisms** (e.g., role-based permissions) to mitigate unintended exposure.

The **model-driven architecture (MDA) for interactive digital twin cockpits** (Dalibor et al., 2020) does not explicitly mention privacy, but its **safety-critical design principles** imply that data flows must be auditable and tamper-proof, reinforcing the need for **end-to-end encryption** and **immutable logging** to protect data integrity and privacy during real-time operations.
"
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
  **Standardization:**
  The provided documents reference the **ISO 23247 – Digital Twin Framework for Manufacturing** standard (2021), which is the most relevant industry standard for Digital Twin (DT) characteristics in manufacturing. The standard is structured into four parts, with **Part 2 (Reference Architecture)** being the most critical for defining DT entities and their interactions:

  - **Relevant Components**:
    1. **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**: Represents physical entities like equipment, facilities, products, personnel, or processes that have observable physical presence/operation in manufacturing.
    2. **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**: Manages data collection from OMEs and controls their operations.
    3. **Digital Twin Entity**: The digital representation of the OME, capturing its state and behavior.
    4. **User Entity**: Provides interfaces for user interaction (e.g., monitoring, controlling OMEs).
    5. **Functional Entities (FEs)**: Smaller units within entities responsible for specific tasks (e.g., actuation, data transformation, user interfacing).

  - **Compliance and Adoption**:
    - The standard does not prescribe implementation details for FEs but suggests using frameworks like **OPC Unified Architecture** for interoperability.
    - While ISO 23247 is a foundational standard, it does not explicitly cover lifecycle governance, twinning processes, or decision-making capabilities—these are implied but not detailed in the provided documents.
    - For broader adoption, compliance frameworks may rely on additional industry-specific standards (e.g., automotive sector via [GitHub link](https://github.com/McSCert/Automotive-Digital-Twin) referenced in Document 1).
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The **security and safety considerations** for a Digital Twin system in the provided documents are heavily influenced by **ISO 26262-1:2018 (Functional Safety for Road Vehicles)** and **ISO 15622 (Adaptive Cruise Control, ACC)**. For **cybersecurity protocols**, ISO 26262-1 mandates a **risk-based approach** to cybersecurity, requiring the implementation of **hardened communication stacks** (e.g., CAN bus encryption for ACC systems) and **secure boot processes** to prevent unauthorized modifications. The **3GPP TS 22.185 (v2X services)** specifies **authentication systems** (e.g., **EAP-TLS or X.509 certificates**) for secure peer-to-peer communication between vehicles and infrastructure, ensuring only authorized entities can participate in data exchanges.

For **access control mechanisms**, ISO 26262-1 enforces **strict role-based access control (RBAC)** for safety-critical components, where only certified drivers or authorized service providers can interact with the Digital Twin’s real-time data. The **ISO 15622 standard** further requires **fail-safe mechanisms** (e.g., **redundant sensors, failover protocols**) to ensure system resilience in the event of cyberattacks or hardware failures. The **model-driven architecture (MDA) for interactive digital twin cockpits** (Dalibor et al., 2020) emphasizes **real-time safety validation**, where any deviations from predefined operational boundaries trigger **automated fail-safe responses** (e.g., emergency braking or system shutdown).

Threat mitigation strategies are implicitly addressed in ISO 26262-1 through **formal verification techniques** (e.g., **model checking, static analysis**) to detect and neutralize potential vulnerabilities in software/hardware components. The **3GPP TS 22.185** also mandates **threat modeling** for v2X services, focusing on **man-in-the-middle attacks, replay attacks, and denial-of-service (DoS) risks**, with solutions like **rate-limiting, intrusion detection systems (IDS), and dynamic key rotation**. The absence of explicit details in the documents implies reliance on **industry best practices** for **zero-trust architecture** and **continuous security monitoring** to maintain compliance with evolving threats.
"
]
